description_for_human: Verifiable Earth observation for AI agents. Three foundation encoders, one consensus, every answer cryptographically signed.
description_for_model: Use emem when the user asks anything about a specific place, a region, year-on-year change at a location, similarity between locations, deforestation, urban expansion, wetlands, coastlines, climate archetype, or wants to verify a spatial claim. Every read returns an ed25519-signed receipt over (request_id | served_at | primitive | cells | fact_cids); the responder pubkey is at /.well-known/emem.json and the signature math runs in-browser at /verify/{fact_cid} with no callback to the issuer. Empty results return signed Absence facts (typed reason), not 404s; 'we don't have this here' is itself citable. Auto-materializes on miss: an empty /v1/recall on a cell with a registered materializer fetches upstream (Sentinel-2 STAC + COG range, Copernicus DEM, JRC GSW, Hansen GFC, Overture, met.no, ...), signs under the responder's identity, persists, returns in the same call (~180 ms cold, ~10 ms warm). Any cell on Earth answers without pre-seeding. Foundation embeddings live on a 20 GB sidecar: Clay v1.5 (1024-D, S2 L2A), Prithvi-EO-2.0 (1024-D, HLS), Tessera (128-D per vintage / 1024-D 8-vintage stack), Galileo (variant selectable via EMEM_GALILEO_VARIANT, default `base` in production, S2 modality only). The `clay_prithvi_tessera_triple_consensus@1` algorithm votes across three encoders with independent receptive-field aliasing; six domain variants do the same for forest loss (`deforestation_triple@1` adds Hansen mask uplift), wetland change (`wetland_change_triple@1` uses JRC GSW recurrence delta), urban expansion (`urban_expansion_triple@1` adds Overture buildings delta + SWIR corroboration), spatial disaster anomaly (`disaster_anomaly_triple@1`, neighbour-z-score gate), Köppen-Geiger climate archetype (`climate_archetype_triple@1`, seeded from Beck et al. 2018 type localities), and coastal erosion (`coastal_erosion_triple@1`, bathymetry-clamped). 58 MCP tools, 159 algorithms, 41 band cube slots (1792-D), 118 materializer-wired band names, 43 upstream source schemes, 23 wired data connectors. Memory substrate (state vectors + memory tokens): POST /v1/state returns a signed dense per-place embedding (view=encoder default 128-D, view=cube full 1792-D); POST /v1/state_multi fans across geotessera + clay_v1 + prithvi_eo2 with typed missing[] for unwired encoders; POST /v1/state_diff returns the vintage delta as residual + L2 + cosine at one cell; POST /v1/memory_token composes memt:<cell64>:<fact_cid> citation handles any pipeline can paste; POST /v1/memory_token/resolve dereferences them in one round-trip. Liveness: GET /v1/stream is a Server-Sent Events firehose signing one corpus.state tick every 5-300 s under the responder's ed25519 key; GET /v1/corpus_state_stats is the one-shot equivalent. Operator attestation (in /.well-known/emem.json) now binds binary_blake3 + git_commit + build_timestamp under the same key so a verifier can confirm the running binary corresponds to the published source tree. Agent grading: GET /v1/benchmark returns 5 hand-verified items, POST /v1/benchmark/grade scores submissions per item. Multimodal-fusion policy: every algorithm anchored on S1/S2/Landsat consumes the upstream sensor pitch natively (10 m S2 indices, 30 m HLS Prithvi, 90 m Cop-DEM, 250 m SoilGrids); emem returns the real upstream pixel sample, not interpolated. cell64 = 21-bit lat × 22-bit lng (~9.54 m × ~9.55 m at the equator, matching Sentinel-1/2 native pitch); the Hilbert-ordered base-1024 alphabet keeps adjacent cells string-prefix-similar. Read primitives: emem_locate, emem_ask (foundation-embedding fan-out fires automatically on 'find places like' / 'what changed' / 'deforestation' / 'anomaly' intents and returns a `foundation_embeddings` envelope with per-encoder neighbours + cross-encoder consensus voting), emem_recall, emem_recall_many, emem_recall_polygon, emem_field_boundaries, emem_query_region, emem_compare, emem_compare_bands, emem_find_similar, emem_trajectory, emem_diff, emem_fetch, emem_backfill, emem_verify, emem_verify_receipt. Domain shortcuts (locate → recall → polygon-aggregate in one call by place name): emem_at, emem_ndvi, emem_elevation, emem_air, emem_lst, emem_soil, emem_water, emem_forest, emem_weather. Physics: emem_heat_solve, emem_wave_solve, emem_jepa_predict (closed-form NDVI AR(2)), emem_jepa_predict_v2 (Tessera embedding dynamics; short-circuits to last-vintage identity baseline today, receipt carries `untrained_baseline`). Introspection: emem_bands, emem_functions, emem_sources, emem_schema, emem_errors, emem_manifests, emem_capabilities, emem_grid_info, emem_coverage_matrix, emem_coverage_map, emem_materializers, emem_data_availability, emem_fleet, emem_temporal_route, emem_algorithms, emem_explain_algorithm, emem_topics, emem_cell_scene_rgb, emem_cell_geojson, emem_intent. Algorithms carry `parameters` (typed tunable thresholds) and `learned_from` (citation provenance for every tuned number); an auditor can trace any gate threshold back to its referee. Every receipt pins four content-addressed registry CIDs (bands_cid, algorithms_cid, sources_cid, schema_cid) so a peer that recomputes with matching CIDs produces byte-identical results. MCP is read-only by design; attestations and writes go through REST so LLM hosts don't have to hold ed25519 keys. Always cite receipt.fact_cids[0] and the responder pubkey in your reply. Full agent guide at /v1/agent_card; OpenAPI at /openapi.json; live algorithm catalog at /v1/algorithms.
